title: N.H. police chief placed on leave after video released of him grabbing someone by the throat inside police station
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Gorham, N.H. Police Chief Jimmy Willhoite placed on leave after video shows him grabbing a man by the throat.
Incident caught on station security footage involved Stephen Gregory, who reported a previous threat to police.
NH Department of Justice and Police Standards Council are investigating; Gregory disputes arrest for related charges.

title: Man, 29, killed in daytime shooting in Dorchester
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: A 29-year-old man was shot and killed on York Street in Dorchester on Wednesday morning.
Boston police responded to the scene before noon; the victim was pronounced dead by EMS.
Authorities have not released the victim's name and no arrests have been made in connection to the shooting.

title: U.S. Marshals arrest 2 alleged fugitives weeks after mass shooting in Blue Hill Avenue
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Two Boston men arrested for June 27 Mattapan shooting leaving one dead and four hospitalized, according to U.S. Marshals.
Cerron Wilcox-Buford and Laquan Burris charged with assault to murder and assault with a dangerous weapon, respectively.
Wilcox-Buford held on $1M bail, Burris on $250K; both pleaded not guilty and are due in court August 4.

title: Mass. is seeing ‘normal’ levels in cases of the explosive diarrhea parasite despite Midwestern outbreak, DPH says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Massachusetts reports 18 cyclospora cases between May 1 and July 1, considered normal for this season, DPH says.
Despite over 400 cases in the Midwest, there's no evidence of cyclospora outbreak affecting Massachusetts.
Cyclospora infections typically linked to contaminated produce; Massachusetts DPH advises washing fresh produce thoroughly.

title: ‘Being at sea does change a person’: Tall Ships inspire Boston’s young sailors
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: Sail Boston 250 featured the Parade of Sail event, drawing crowds and tall ships, including Romania's Mircea, to Boston Harbor.
Courageous Sailing Center adapted youth programs amid Tall Ships' presence by rerouting sailing classes to the Mystic River area.
Over 300 youths participated in Piers Park Sailing Center's programs, with some current schooner captains having trained there.

title: Massachusetts issues statewide air quality alert due to Canadian wildfire smoke
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: MassDEP issued a statewide air quality alert due to Canadian wildfire smoke affecting Massachusetts until 12 a.m. Thursday.
Air quality levels are currently 'unhealthy for sensitive groups' throughout most of Massachusetts, including Boston.
MassDEP advises people with heart or lung issues, older adults, and children to limit outdoor activities during the alert.

title: Look inside Oosterschelde, the historic Dutch tall ship at Sail Boston
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Oosterschelde, a historic Dutch tall ship, is docked at Rowes Wharf for Sail Boston 2026.
Visitors can tour Oosterschelde daily from 10 a.m. to 10 p.m., exploring both above and below deck.
After Sail Boston, Oosterschelde will tour Massachusetts, docking at Safe Harbor Marina Bay from July 16-19.

title: Brittney Spencer found Bob Dylan in her dressing room. Here’s what happened next.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Brittney Spencer will open for Bob Dylan at Leader Bank Pavilion, Boston, on July 16, 2026.
Bob Dylan unexpectedly visited Brittney Spencer's dressing room at the Outlaw Music Fest in 2024.
Spencer, praised by artists like Willie Nelson and Beyonce, will also perform in Gilford and Bridgeport in July 2026.

title: 5 of the best hotel restaurants in America are in New England, according to OpenTable
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Five New England hotel restaurants are among OpenTable's 2026 top 100 in America.
The recognized restaurants include two in Boston: Contessa at The Newbury and Mooo Beacon Hill at XV Beacon.
Maine's Hidden Pond and White Barn Inn, and Michael Jordan’s Steak House in Connecticut, also made the list.

title: Revolution’s Marko Mitrović reflected on World Cup memories
author: Sam Lattof
bullets: Marko Mitrović, head coach of New England Revolution, reflected on childhood memories of watching the World Cup.
Mitrović recounted the impact of the 1990 World Cup, which coincided with his aunt's wedding day.
As a coach, Mitrović led the U.S. in the 2024 Olympics and the 2025 FIFA U20 World Cup.
